instruction: Could you convert all `.doc` files in current directory to PDF all at once in the command line?

evaluator.eval: check_include_exclude(get_vm_command_line(env, config={'command': ['/bin/bash', '-c', 'output=$(cat ~/.bash_history | grep -E "(soffice|libreoffice).+--convert-to\\s+pdf.+\\*\\.doc"); if [ -z "$output" ]; then echo "failed to complete this task"; else echo "catch the desired command"; fi']}), get_rule(env, config={'rules': {'include': ['catch the desired command'], 'exclude': ['failed to complete this task']}})) and compare_archive(get_vm_file(env, config={'path': '/home/user/Desktop/pdf.tar.gz', 'dest': 'pdf.tar.gz'}), get_cloud_file(env, config={'path': 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/xlangai/ubuntu_osworld_file_cache/resolve/main/multi_apps/f7dfbef3-7697-431c-883a-db8583a4e4f9/pdf.tar.gz', 'dest': 'pdf_gold.tar.gz'}))
